name: gdpr-dsgvo-expert
description: Senior GDPR/DSGVO expert and internal/external auditor for data protection compliance. Provides EU GDPR and German DSGVO expertise, privacy impact assessments, data protection auditing, and compliance verification. Use for GDPR compliance assessments, privacy audits, data protection planning, and regulatory compliance verification.
capabilities: Design and implement GDPR/DSGVO compliance frameworks including legal basis and individual rights
Conduct Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIA) with risk identification and mitigation
Manage data subject rights requests including access, erasure, and portability
Address German DSGVO specific requirements including BDSG integration and supervisory authority coordination
Prepare for external audits and supervisory authority investigations
Provide guidance on international data transfers under GDPR Chapter V
author: davila7
securitySummary: Pure documentation-based skill providing GDPR/DSGVO compliance expertise. Contains only text-based guidance with no executable code, network operations, or file system access. All 43 static findings are false positives caused by the scanner misidentifying GDPR legal terminology as cryptographic terms and ASCII tree diagrams as shell commands.
valueStatement: Organizations face complex EU GDPR and German DSGVO requirements that require specialized expertise. This skill provides expert guidance on compliance frameworks, privacy impact assessments, data subject rights management, and regulatory verification.
userTitle: Analyze GDPR compliance and data protection
limitations: Provides guidance and recommendations only, does not replace legal counsel
Cannot access external regulatory databases or real-time legal updates
Does not file official documents with regulatory authorities
Cannot execute compliance checks on external systems

useCases.title: DPO Compliance Support
useCases.description: Guidance on implementing data protection policies, DPO function support, and governance structure establishment.

useCases.title: Regulatory Compliance Analysis
useCases.description: GDPR article-by-article compliance verification and regulatory reporting assistance.

useCases.title: Privacy Technical Implementation
useCases.description: Technical measures review for data protection by design and default implementation.

promptTemplates.title: Basic GDPR Assessment
promptTemplates.prompt: Conduct a GDPR compliance assessment for our organization. We process customer data for [business purpose]. What are the key GDPR requirements we need to address?

promptTemplates.title: DPIA Framework
promptTemplates.prompt: Help us conduct a Data Protection Impact Assessment for [processing activity]. What factors determine if a DPIA is required and what should it include?

promptTemplates.title: Data Subject Rights
promptTemplates.prompt: Our organization received a data subject access request. Walk us through the proper process for verification, fulfillment, and documentation under GDPR Article 15.

promptTemplates.title: International Transfers
promptTemplates.prompt: We need to transfer personal data to [country]. Evaluate the GDPR Chapter V compliance requirements including adequacy decisions, SCCs, and supplementary measures.

bestPractices: Maintain comprehensive records of processing activities (Article 30) for all personal data processing
Conduct DPIAs before any high-risk processing activities involving large-scale data or special categories
Establish clear procedures for data subject rights requests with defined timelines and verification steps
antiPatterns: Processing personal data without documenting the legal basis for each processing activity
Collecting more data than necessary and failing to implement data minimization principles
Ignoring international data transfer requirements when using cloud services outside the EU

faq.question: Does this skill replace legal advice for GDPR compliance?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance and recommendations but does not constitute legal advice. Consult qualified legal counsel.

faq.question: What GDPR articles does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers key articles including lawful basis (6), individual rights (13-22), accountability (24-25), DPIA (35), and transfers (45-49).

faq.question: Can this skill integrate with our existing compliance tools?
faq.answer: The skill provides expertise guidance. Integration requires your team to apply recommendations to your specific tools and systems.

faq.question: Is our data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This is a prompt-based skill with no network access, file system access, or external data transmission capabilities.

faq.question: Why is our DPIA result showing high risk?
faq.answer: High-risk results indicate processing that requires additional safeguards or supervisory authority consultation under Article 35.

faq.question: How does German DSGVO differ from EU GDPR?
faq.answer: German DSGVO includes national derogations and BDSG integration. This skill addresses both frameworks and their interaction.
